title: HR Turnover Prediction (Flight Risk Engine)
summary: An XGBoost predictive engine identifying high-risk employees with ~70% recall. Features SHAP explainability to translate machine learning into actionable executive strategy.

details.subtitle: 1. The Imbalance Trap
details.content: Standard ML models fail in HR by defaulting to a 50% threshold, ignoring severe class imbalance. We trained a Baseline vs. Weighted XGBoost model to demonstrate how penalizing the majority class reshapes predictions.

details.subtitle: 2. Threshold Optimization
details.content: Missing a quitter costs ~$15,000, while checking on a happy employee costs a $10 cup of coffee. We mathematically shifted the decision threshold down to 25%, maximizing business ROI and capturing ~70% of actual flight risks.

details.subtitle: 3. Executive SHAP Insights
details.content: Extracted global feature importance using SHAP to prove mathematically to HR leadership that 'OverTime' (Burnout) and 'Stock Option Levels' (Golden Handcuffs) are much stronger attrition drivers than base salary.

title: Employees Absence Prediction
summary: Using Python, Pandas, and XGBoost with EWMA lag features to predict employee absences for April 2026, assisting leadership in proactive workforce planning.

details.subtitle: The Architecture
details.content: Engineered a robust time-series pipeline using Pandas to build Exponentially Weighted Moving Average (EWMA) lag features, feeding into an optimized XGBoost regressor. The model captured complex seasonal sickness trends.

details.subtitle: Macro Prediction
details.content: The model successfully forecasted overall headcount absences across all 8 manufacturing departments with high accuracy on the out-of-time holdout data.

details.subtitle: Micro Prediction
details.content: Further granular analysis allowed the system to predict specific illness volumes (e.g., Dyspepsia, Typhoid) to assist the onsite medical clinic with inventory planning.

title: Wishful Bazaar Dashboard
summary: An interactive, executive-level Tableau dashboard tracking core retail KPIs, sales trends, and profitability metrics for the Wishful Bazaar dataset.

details.subtitle: 1. Trend & Granular Analysis
details.content: Incorporated time-series analysis and categorical breakdowns to allow category managers to drill down into specific product lines and identify seasonal purchasing trends.

details.subtitle: 2. Interactive Data Discovery
details.content: Implemented dynamic filtering and cross-highlighting, empowering non-technical business users to actively explore the data and answer their own ad-hoc queries without writing SQL.

details.subtitle: 3. The Executive Dashboard
details.content: Stakeholders needed a centralized, interactive view of overarching retail performance. Designed a top-down Tableau dashboard focusing on immediate visibility into revenue streams and profit margins.

title: 360-Degree Auto-Reporting Engine
summary: An end-to-end Python pipeline utilizing python-pptx to dynamically generate customized, executive-ready PowerPoint slide decks from a master dataset.

details.subtitle: 1. The Transformation
details.content: Manually generating individual executive review decks requires days of tedious formatting and chart manipulation. This pipeline automates the transformation of raw, messy spreadsheets into actionable insights instantly.

details.subtitle: 2. The Executive Output
details.content: Engineered a script to calculate complex benchmarking logic and dynamically inject ranked text, KPIs, and clustered charts into native PowerPoint objects. The result is a pixel-perfect, designer-quality slide.

details.subtitle: 3. The Automation Scale
details.content: The engine scales effortlessly across the entire company roster. It programmatically generates customized charts, maintains layout integrity, and compiles all individual reports into a single, perfectly formatted Master Deck.

title: PDS Extraction Engine
summary: Automated ETL pipeline using xlwings and Pandas to extract, clean, and aggregate employee performance data from hundreds of unstructured Excel files.

details.subtitle: Unstructured Data Parsing
details.content: Bypasses manual entry by programmatically scanning and extracting specific KPI metrics across hundreds of individual, variably-formatted performance review sheets.

title: Dynamic QR Generator
summary: Engineered a dynamic Python utility for bulk QR code generation, automating trackable links for HR surveys, event registrations, and internal campaigns.

details.subtitle: The Context
details.content: The HR department needed hundreds of custom, trackable QR codes for employee surveys and event registrations, but manual graphic design generation was causing severe operational bottlenecks.

details.subtitle: The Engine
details.content: Built a scalable Python script using the 'qrcode' and 'Pillow' libraries to automate bulk generation, leveraging multi-threading to process entire Excel databases in under a second.

details.subtitle: High-Fidelity Outcome
details.content: The tool mathematically calculates aspect ratios for logo injection and processes alpha channels (transparency), instantly rendering pixel-perfect, branded assets ready for distribution.

title: Portfolio_OS
summary: A high-performance, cyberpunk-themed portfolio mainframe built to translate complex human resource data into tactical clarity.

details.subtitle: The Context
details.content: Standard CVs and portfolios lack the visceral, interactive depth necessary to truly showcase a technical skillset.

details.subtitle: The Architecture
details.content: Engineered a Next.js frontend with Tailwind CSS and Framer Motion for extreme Cyberpunk aesthetics, featuring custom React hooks for scroll transparency and mobile touch state bypassing.

details.subtitle: The Outcome
details.content: A high-performance, responsive digital terminal that serves as both a portfolio and a technical demonstration.

title: IBM Attrition: Survival Analysis
summary: Applied Kaplan-Meier estimators and Cox Proportional Hazards models to IBM's HR dataset to calculate precise time-to-event attrition risks.

details.subtitle: 1. Time-to-Event Modeling
details.content: Standard classification predicts IF someone will leave. By utilizing the 'lifelines' library to build a Cox Proportional Hazards (CPH) model, I calculated exactly WHEN they are likely to leave and what variables accelerate that timeline.

details.subtitle: 2. The 'Overtime' Hazard Ratio
details.content: The mathematical modeling proved that Overtime is the absolute highest flight risk driver. The CPH model revealed a Hazard Ratio of 3.25, meaning employees working overtime are over 3 times more likely to quit than their peers.

details.subtitle: 3. Interactive & Code Hub
details.content: Translated the complex statistical survival models into an interactive, self-service Looker Studio dashboard, while maintaining the rigorous mathematical engine in a dedicated Python repository.
